footer.title: Take your own notes — for free
footer.description: PokerReads is a mobile-first PWA for live poker players. Save reads, calculate odds, and structure your hands with AI.

potOdds.title: Pot Odds Calculator
potOdds.metaDescription: Calculate minimum required equity based on pot odds to make profitable calls in poker.
potOdds.howTo: How to use the Pot Odds Calculator
potOdds.desc1: Pot odds represent the ratio of the current size of the pot to the cost of a contemplated call. Knowing your pot odds is crucial for making mathematically profitable decisions in poker.
potOdds.li1: Current Pot Size: The total amount of money in the pot, including the bets placed in the current round.
potOdds.li2: Bet to Call: The amount you need to put into the pot to stay in the hand.
potOdds.desc2: The required equity tells you how often your hand needs to win to break even. If your estimated chance of winning (hand equity) is higher than the required equity, calling is mathematically profitable (+EV) in the long run.

pushFold.title: Push/Fold Nash Chart
pushFold.metaDescription: Optimal Push/Fold ranges based on Nash Equilibrium for short-stacked tournament poker.
pushFold.desc1: Push/Fold is an unexploitable strategy (Nash Equilibrium) used in short-stack tournament play (typically 15 Big Blinds or less). When you have a short stack, you lose your leverage to play post-flop profitably.
pushFold.desc2: Instead of limping or minimally raising, the optimal mathematical play is often to simply push All-in or Fold. This chart approximates profitable shoving ranges based on stack depth and table position.

icm.title: ICM Calculator
icm.metaDescription: Independent Chip Model (ICM) calculator for final tables and sit & gos.
icm.desc1: The Independent Chip Model (ICM) calculates a mathematical evaluation of a player's chip stack based on the payout structure of a tournament. It helps determine the real monetary value of your chips at any given moment.
icm.desc2: This is especially important at the final table or near the bubble, when chip values become heavily decoupled from linearly equivalent dollars. Players use ICM to negotiate equitable chops or evaluate decisions with high payout variance.

equity.title: Hand Equity Calculator
equity.metaDescription: Calculate the win % between two or more poker hands at any street — preflop, flop, turn or river.
equity.hint: Pick at least two complete hands to see equities. The board is optional.
equity.howTo: How to use the Equity Calculator
equity.desc1: Equity is the share of the pot a hand expects to win, given the cards still to come. Knowing it for hand-vs-hand spots is essential for studying all-in decisions, semi-bluffs and post-flop confrontations.
equity.li1: Pick the hole cards for two or more players. The calculator supports up to 9 hands at once.
equity.li2: Optionally lock in the flop, turn or river to see how equity shifts as the board runs out.
equity.desc2: Post-flop scenarios are evaluated exhaustively over every remaining runout. Preflop and other large samples use Monte Carlo simulation (around 8,000 trials), accurate to roughly ±0.5%.

breakEven.title: Break-Even & MDF Calculator
breakEven.metaDescription: Calculate the break-even percentage for bluffs and the Minimum Defense Frequency (MDF) to protect your range.
breakEven.howTo: How to use the Break-Even & MDF Calculator
breakEven.desc1: Every bet you make — whether a value bet or a bluff — has a break-even point. The break-even percentage tells you how often your bet needs to work (opponent folds) for it to be immediately profitable as a pure bluff.
breakEven.li1: The percentage of the time your bluff must succeed to break even. Calculated as Bet / (Pot + Bet).
breakEven.li2: Minimum Defense Frequency — the portion of your range you must continue with (call or raise) to prevent the opponent from profiting with any two cards.
breakEven.desc2: MDF = 1 − Break-Even%. If you fold more than the MDF, your opponent can bluff any two cards at a profit. Use this alongside pot odds to build balanced, unexploitable ranges.
